data.symptoms: Sudden severe abdominal pain; colicky or sharp; vomiting; constipation; abdominal distension; failure to pass flatus; peritonitis signs
data.treatment_drugs: IV fluids (resuscitation); NGT; urinary catheter; broad-spectrum antibiotics (Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole) if perforation suspected; urgent surgery evaluation

data.symptoms: Cough; runny nose; sore throat; low-grade fever; nasal congestion; malaise
data.treatment_drugs: Supportive care (fluids; rest); Paracetamol for fever/pain; No antibiotics unless secondary bacterial infection confirmed

data.symptoms: Pallor (pale skin/palms); fatigue; shortness of breath; dizziness; rapid heartbeat; spoon-shaped nails (koilonychia)
data.treatment_drugs: Ferrous sulphate (Iron); Folic acid; Albendazole (to treat hookworm cause); diet high in green leafy vegetables and meat

data.symptoms: Recurrent wheezing; shortness of breath; chest tightness; cough (worse at night or early morning); history of allergies
data.treatment_drugs: Salbutamol (Inhaler or Nebulised) for relief; Inhaled Corticosteroids (Beclomethasone) for prevention; Prednisolone (oral) for acute attacks

data.symptoms: Oral: White non-adherent patches on tongue/cheeks; Vaginal: Itching; thick curd-like discharge; redness; pain on urination
data.treatment_drugs: Nystatin suspension or Miconazole gel (Oral); Clotrimazole or Fluconazole (Vaginal/Systemic)

data.symptoms: Profuse 'rice-water' watery diarrhoea; effortless vomiting; rapid dehydration (sunken eyes; dry mouth; slow skin pinch); leg cramps
data.treatment_drugs: Aggressive Rehydration (ORS for mild; IV Ringer's Lactate for severe); Ciprofloxacin or Azithromycin (single dose after rehydration)

data.symptoms: Excessive thirst (polydipsia); frequent urination (polyuria); unexplained weight loss; blurred vision; slow-healing sores; fatigue
data.treatment_drugs: Metformin (Type 2); Gliclazide; Insulin (Type 1 or advanced Type 2); Lifestyle: regular exercise and low-sugar diet

data.symptoms: Loose stools ≥3 times/day; Dysentery: Blood or mucus in stool; abdominal cramps; fever; tenesmus (straining)
data.treatment_drugs: ORS (Rehydration); Zinc sulphate (especially for children); Ciprofloxacin (for Dysentery); Metronidazole (if amoebic suspected)

data.symptoms: Heartburn (burning chest pain after meals); acid regurgitation; upper abdominal discomfort; chronic cough; hoarse voice
data.treatment_drugs: Antacids (Magnesium trisilicate); Omeprazole (PPI); Lifestyle: avoid large late-night meals and lying down after eating

data.symptoms: Unexplained weight loss; chronic fever >1 month; persistent diarrhoea; generalized lymphadenopathy; recurrent infections (skin; respiratory)
data.treatment_drugs: Antiretroviral Therapy (ART - e.g. TLD); Cotrimoxazole (prophylaxis); Management of Opportunistic Infections

data.symptoms: Often asymptomatic ('silent killer'); occasionally headache; blurred vision; dizziness; chest pain; high blood pressure readings (≥140/90)
data.treatment_drugs: Hydrochlorothiazide (Thiazide); Amlodipine (CCB); Enalapril (ACEi); Losartan (ARB); lifestyle: low salt and weight management

data.symptoms: Fever; chills/rigors; headache; joint pains; nausea; vomiting; confirmed by RDT or blood slide; Severe: confusion; seizures; severe anaemia
data.treatment_drugs: Artemether-Lumefantrine (Co-artemether) for uncomplicated; IV Artesunate for severe; Paracetamol for fever

data.symptoms: High fever; severe headache; neck stiffness; photophobia (light sensitivity); confusion; vomiting; non-blanching rash (sometimes)
data.treatment_drugs: IV Antibiotics (Ceftriaxone + Benzylpenicillin); Dexamethasone; Urgent hospitalization and supportive care

data.symptoms: Burning epigastric pain (often related to hunger or specific foods); bloating; nausea; vomiting; 'hunger pains'
data.treatment_drugs: H. Pylori Eradication: Omeprazole + Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin (Triple Therapy for 14 days)

data.symptoms: Cough; fever; fast breathing; chest indrawing; chest pain (pleuritic); grunting or shortness of breath; crackles on auscultation
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin (Oral) for mild; Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin (IV) for severe; Oxygen if SpO2 is low

data.symptoms: Intense itching (worse at night); small raised zig-zag burrows; rash between fingers; on wrists; navel; and genitals
data.treatment_drugs: Permethrin 5% cream (wash off after 8-12 hours) or Benzyl Benzoate (BBP) 25% emulsion; treat all household members

data.symptoms: Urethral/Vaginal discharge; painful urination; genital ulcers or sores; lower abdominal pain; inguinal swelling (buboes)
data.treatment_drugs: Syndromic Management: Ceftriaxone (IM) + Doxycycline + Metronidazole; Partner notification and treatment

data.symptoms: Localised redness; warmth; swelling; pain; Abscess: Fluctuant tender mass (pus); fever
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin or Amoxicillin-Clavulanic Acid; Abscess: Incision & Drainage (I&D) is mandatory; warm compresses

data.symptoms: Sudden onset focal deficit: Facial droop; Arm weakness (one side); Speech difficulty (slurring); blurred vision; severe headache
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin (initially); BP management; CT Brain to distinguish Ischaemic vs Haemorrhagic; acute hospitalization and rehabilitation

data.symptoms: Chronic cough >2 weeks; night sweats; fever; unexplained weight loss; fatigue; coughing up blood (haemoptysis)
data.treatment_drugs: 2HRZE / 4HR (Standard 6-month regimen: Rifampicin; Isoniazid; Pyrazinamide; Ethambutol)

data.symptoms: Persistent high fever; abdominal pain; headache; malaise; constipation or 'pea-soup' diarrhoea; enlarged spleen; 'rose spots' on chest
data.treatment_drugs: Ciprofloxacin or Azithromycin or Ceftriaxone; ensure safe water and food hygiene

data.symptoms: Painful urination (dysuria); frequency; urgency; cloudy or foul-smelling urine; suprapubic pain; Loin pain/fever if Pyelonephritis
data.treatment_drugs: Nitrofurantoin or Ciprofloxacin or Amoxicillin; increased oral fluid intake
